perturbation.original_text: Bravatek agrees to serve as a non-exclusive project sales lead finder for COMPANY. In this capacity, Bravatek will use its best efforts to provide the following services to COMPANY. a. Promote, market and introduce the Products to prospective clients in the government space nationwide.
perturbation.changed_text: Bravatek agrees to serve as an exclusive project sales lead finder for COMPANY. In this capacity, Bravatek will use its best efforts to provide the following services to COMPANY. a. Promote, market and introduce the Products to prospective clients in the government space nationwide.
perturbation.explanation: This change replaces 'non-exclusive' with 'exclusive,' creating a contradiction. The original agreement allows COMPANY to work with other lead finders, while the modified text implies Bravatek is the only authorized lead finder. This contradicts the later clauses in Section 2(a), where COMPANY agrees to list Bravatek as a 'non-exclusive alliance partner', this clause now contradicts the exclusivity granted to Bravatek in Section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Duties of COMPANY COMPANY agrees to use its best efforts to promote and support project lead finding and after-sales support of Bravatek by: a. Listing Bravatek in all appropriate sales and marketing materials as a non-exclusive alliance partner (with focus of government customers) b. Provide timely responses to both technical and administrative questions posed by Bravatek. c. Promote Bravatek's product and service offerings whenever possible. d. Aid Bravatek in the writing of any technical/marketing/sales documents when requested and participate in mutually-agreed upon sales calls. e. Provide Bravatek with co-branded marketing material that can be emailed or handed to prospective clients.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'non-exclusive' to 'exclusive' in the first paragraph creates a direct contradiction with the clause in 'Duties of COMPANY' that requires listing Bravatek as a 'non-exclusive alliance partner.' The modified text grants Bravatek exclusivity, rendering the 'non-exclusive' designation in the contradicted text factually incorrect and creating an in-text contradiction. This aligns with the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition, where key terms are defined inconsistently.

perturbation.original_text: For any Product or Solution sold to any perspective clients introduced by Bravatek registered with COMPANY via email to COMPANY's CEO and delivered through Bravatek or a COMPANY-designated distribution affiliate(s) or sales channel(s), Bravatek will receive a lead-finder fee, to be mutually discussed and finally decided by COMPANY at the range of minimum of 10% to maximum of 20% of project revenue, with an exact fee to be depending upon the overall project sales margin and cost of development and delivery of each project, payable NET 30 days after each client payment on delivered products received at COMPANY's bank account.
perturbation.changed_text: For any Product or Solution sold to any perspective clients introduced by Bravatek registered with COMPANY via email to COMPANY's CEO and delivered through Bravatek or a COMPANY-designated distribution affiliate(s) or sales channel(s), Bravatek will receive a lead-finder fee of 5% of project revenue, payable NET 90 days after each client payment on delivered products received at COMPANY's bank account.
perturbation.explanation: This change modifies the compensation terms, creating a conflict. The original text specifies that Bravatek will receive a fee within the range of 10% to 20% which is 'mutually discussed and finally decided by COMPANY'. The modified text defines a fixed 5% fee, eliminating the range and mutual agreement aspect, and changing the payment terms to NET 90 days. This contradicts the earlier statement about the fee amount being mutually discussed and potentially higher, creating an in-text contradiction about what Bravatek is entitled to. Now the amount is pre-determined and there is no agreement being needed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: When custom Products are designed, developed and to be delivered to Bravatek-identified perspective clients, the parties shall agree to a proposed sales price for use during the project in writing prior to the commencement of each project. 2 For any Product or Solution sold to any perspective clients introduced by Bravatek registered with COMPANY via email to COMPANY's CEO and delivered through Bravatek or a COMPANY-designated distribution affiliate(s) or sales channel(s), Bravatek will receive a lead-finder fee of 5% of project revenue, payable NET 90 days after each client payment on delivered products received at COMPANY's bank account.
perturbation.justification: The changed text specifies a fixed 5% lead-finder fee, eliminating the previously stipulated range of 10%-20% and the requirement of mutual agreement with COMPANY to determine the exact fee amount. This contradicts the initial agreement that the fee would be 'mutually discussed and finally decided by COMPANY at the range of minimum of 10% to maximum of 20% of project revenue'. By predetermining the fee at 5% and changing the payment terms to NET 90, a clear contradiction is established, making the contract inconsistent and potentially unenforceable. The contradicted text occurs because the changed text appears in the same place as the original text, causing a change to the same section of the contract. This change contradicts the intent of a prior mutual agreement, meaning it is still considered an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement is twelve (12) months from the date hereof, and will be automatically renewed for one (1) additional twelve month period unless either party shall notify the other in writing of its intention not to renew. Such notice must be given ninety (90) days prior to expiration of the original term. This Agreement may also be terminated by either party upon ninety (90) days written notice.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement is twelve (12) months from the date hereof, and will be automatically renewed for one (1) additional twelve month period unless either party shall notify the other in writing of its intention not to renew. Such notice must be given thirty (30) days prior to expiration of the original term. This Agreement may also be terminated by either party upon ninety (90) days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: This change modifies the notice period for non-renewal from ninety (90) days to thirty (30) days. This creates a contradiction with the subsequent sentence which says 'This Agreement may also be terminated by either party upon ninety (90) days written notice.' Now there are two notice periods that contradict each other depending if the intent is to terminate or not renew the Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term The term of this Agreement is twelve (12) months from the date hereof, and will be automatically renewed for one (1) additional twelve month period unless either party shall notify the other in writing of its intention not to renew. Such notice must be given thirty (30) days prior to expiration of the original term. This Agreement may also be terminated by either party upon ninety (90) days written notice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the notice period for non-renewal to 30 days, while the agreement also states a 90-day notice period for termination. This directly contradicts the contradicted text. The contradiction stems from having two different notice periods within the agreement, making it unclear which notice period applies and under what circumstances.
